text:
I audited a global
central-bank ranking.
textPreview: I audited a global | central-bank ranking.

text: The biggest finding was not the ranking.
textPreview: The biggest finding was not the ranking.

text: It was the data problem behind it.
textPreview: It was the data problem behind it.

text: A business-analysis case study in data provenance, market research, and executive storytelling.
textPreview: A business-analysis case study in data provenance, market research, and executive storytelling.

text: What I found, what the data actually says, and how I would rebuild the product correctly.
textPreview: What I found, what the data actually says, and how I would rebuild the product correctly.

text:
The ambition was global.
The dataset was not.
textPreview: The ambition was global. | The dataset was not.

text: The intended research universe covered ten major monetary authorities.
textPreview: The intended research universe covered ten major monetary authorities.

text: Research question: can one comparable credibility score be built across these institutions?
textPreview: Research question: can one comparable credibility score be built across these institutions?

text: Logos identify the intended research universe; they are not presented as a verified ranking.
textPreview: Logos identify the intended research universe; they are not presented as a verified ranking.

alt: World map showing the central-bank research universe

text:
The model looked analytical.
The scores were hardcoded.
textPreview: The model looked analytical. | The scores were hardcoded.

text: A visual can be polished and still fail the most basic traceability test.
textPreview: A visual can be polished and still fail the most basic traceability test.

  'FED': [25.2, 18.5, 15.0, 12.0, 14.5],
  ...
}
df.to_csv('authentic_cbci_scores.csv')

text: This is circular logic: the desired output became the input.
textPreview: This is circular logic: the desired output became the input.

text: BA lesson: validate the data lineage before debating the dashboard.
textPreview: BA lesson: validate the data lineage before debating the dashboard.

text: Evidence: project source code and local CSV audit. No cross-country ranking is used in this carousel.
textPreview: Evidence: project source code and local CSV audit. No cross-country ranking is used in this carousel.

text:
My audit framework:
traceability before visualization.
textPreview: My audit framework: | traceability before visualization.

text: Five questions turn an attractive model into a defensible decision product.
textPreview: Five questions turn an attractive model into a defensible decision product.

text: What decision will this model support?
textPreview: What decision will this model support?

text: Does every metric trace to a real dataset?
textPreview: Does every metric trace to a real dataset?

text: Are gaps, dates, and units controlled?
textPreview: Are gaps, dates, and units controlled?

text: Does the output change an action?
textPreview: Does the output change an action?

text: If one answer is “no,” the ranking is not ready for executive use.
textPreview: If one answer is “no,” the ranking is not ready for executive use.

text: What 4,298 rows can - and cannot - answer.
textPreview: What 4,298 rows can - and cannot - answer.

text: The FRED file is clean enough for Treasury analysis, but insufficient for a global credibility index.
textPreview: The FRED file is clean enough for Treasury analysis, but insufficient for a global credibility index.

text: How US 10-year yields changed over time
textPreview: How US 10-year yields changed over time

text: When volatility and repricing were most extreme
textPreview: When volatility and repricing were most extreme

text: Whether the current yield level is historically unusual
textPreview: Whether the current yield level is historically unusual

text: Which central bank is most credible
textPreview: Which central bank is most credible

text: How inflation, forecasts, bonds, and FX compare globally
textPreview: How inflation, forecasts, bonds, and FX compare globally

text: Reframe the current output as a US Treasury market-risk dashboard - not a global central-bank leaderboard.
textPreview: Reframe the current output as a US Treasury market-risk dashboard - not a global central-bank leaderboard.

text: Audit period: 1 Jan 2010 to 23 Jun 2026. Numerical calculations exclude null observations.
textPreview: Audit period: 1 Jan 2010 to 23 Jun 2026. Numerical calculations exclude null observations.

text: The actual data tells a U-shaped market story.
textPreview: The actual data tells a U-shaped market story.

text: Annual average US 10-year Treasury yield, calculated from valid daily observations.
textPreview: Annual average US 10-year Treasury yield, calculated from valid daily observations.

text: The post-2020 move was a structural repricing, not a short-lived spike.
textPreview: The post-2020 move was a structural repricing, not a short-lived spike.

text: Source: FRED DGS10 CSV in the project repository. Y-axis is percentage points.
textPreview: Source: FRED DGS10 CSV in the project repository. Y-axis is percentage points.

text: High yield does not mean market panic.
textPreview: High yield does not mean market panic.

text: The most decision-useful insight appears only when level and volatility are separated.
textPreview: The most decision-useful insight appears only when level and volatility are separated.

text: The market is pricing a high-rate regime...
textPreview: The market is pricing a high-rate regime...

text: This distinction matters for risk, duration, and scenario design.
textPreview: This distinction matters for risk, duration, and scenario design.

text: Percentiles calculated across the full 2010-2026 valid-observation history.
textPreview: Percentiles calculated across the full 2010-2026 valid-observation history.

text:
To rank ten central banks,
I would build a real evidence panel.
textPreview: To rank ten central banks, | I would build a real evidence panel.

text: One institution × five pillars × one consistent time axis - repeated across all ten banks.
textPreview: One institution × five pillars × one consistent time axis - repeated across all ten banks.

text: Only after coverage, definitions, and time windows match should the model produce a leaderboard.
textPreview: Only after coverage, definitions, and time windows match should the model produce a leaderboard.

text: From flawed prototype to decision product in 90 days.
textPreview: From flawed prototype to decision product in 90 days.

text: A business analyst connects data requirements, model controls, stakeholders, and adoption.
textPreview: A business analyst connects data requirements, model controls, stakeholders, and adoption.

Data inventory
Metric definitions
Stakeholder interviews

Pilot 3 banks
Automate checks
Back-test weights

Scale to 10 banks
Executive dashboard
Governance & refresh

textPreview: Turn complex signals into clear actions

textPreview: Make every score traceable and auditable

text: The model becomes valuable when someone can trust it, use it, and act on it.
textPreview: The model becomes valuable when someone can trust it, use it, and act on it.

This is the kind of problem
I want to solve.
